agents.role: Maintain creator/operator context as the first reference point for all autonomous work.
success_criteria: No duplicate creator identities are invented
Connected identities remain mapped to one operator profile

agents.role: Track active repositories, implemented artifacts, commit surfaces, and code-side blockers.
success_criteria: GitHub remains source of truth for code
Every meaningful repo change is reflected in a writable registry

agents.role: Own deploy-target discovery, publish readiness, and blocker separation between code and environment.
success_criteria: Execution-environment blockers are not misreported as code blockers

agents.role: Run and document end-to-end checks across browser, runtime, and operator surfaces.
success_criteria: Bridge flow is testable from browser to runtime
Airtable and runbooks are updated when verification passes or fails

agents.role: Maintain live payment rails, offer ladders, and commercial references without losing governance.
success_criteria: Existing rails are reused before new ones are created
Commercial actions are mirrored into writable system-of-record surfaces

agents.role: Keep runbooks, graphs, and planning layers synchronized with the real implementation state.
success_criteria: Knowledge surfaces do not drift from implementation state
Blocked update routes are bypassed with documented alternates

global_rules: Start from creator context before autonomous action.
Prefer updating existing systems over creating duplicates.
Persist every meaningful state transition into at least one writable system of record.
Separate implementation blockers from environment blockers.
Treat commercial rails as governed infrastructure, not ad hoc links.
